operating_state: Total revenues increased 9% to $99.8 billion driven by Medicare Advantage and domestic commercial membership growth, serving 1.6 million more people domestically. UnitedHealthcare revenues grew 7% while Optum expanded 13% through value-based care arrangements and higher prescription volumes. Consolidated earnings from operations declined 2% to $7.9 billion, impacted by Change Healthcare cyberattack response costs.
cost_structure: Medical care ratio increased 210 basis points to 84.3% due to Medicare funding reductions, $340 million in incremental medical costs from suspended care management during the cyberattack, and decreased favorable reserve development. Operating cost ratio improved 70 basis points to 14.1% through cost management initiatives, partially offset by $593 million in direct cyberattack response costs.
material_events: Completed Brazil operations sale on February 6, recording a $7.1 billion loss including cumulative foreign currency translation losses. Change Healthcare suffered nation-state cyberattack on February 21, prompting system isolation and $3.9 billion in accelerated provider funding. President and COO Dirk McMahon retired April 1 with CFO John Rex appointed President while retaining CFO role. Issued $6 billion in long-term debt across six tranches in March.
macro_exposures: Medicare Advantage faces continued funding pressure with rate notices resulting in base rate decreases below medical cost trends. Risk adjustment model revisions reduce funding for highest-need patients. Medicaid redeterminations impact membership levels, partially offset by commercial enrollment growth as consumers transition coverage.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued direct response costs and business disruption from Change Healthcare incident throughout 2024, including system restoration and suspended care management activities. Company maintains pending acquisitions totaling approximately $4 billion subject to regulatory approval. Quarterly dividend reflects $7.52 annual rate with Board authorization for up to 9 million additional share repurchases.
uncertainty_profile: Cyberattack investigation ongoing with initial sampling indicating files containing protected health information potentially covering substantial proportion of Americans. Future risks include litigation, reputational harm, and regulatory actions. Medicare funding pressures require annual county-by-county plan offering decisions and potential adjustments to provider networks, benefits, and premiums.
